title: Supreme Court Rules Right to Walk on Footpaths is a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that the right to walk on footpaths is a fundamental right of citizens. The ruling came against the backdrop of a surge in pedestrian deaths across Indian cities due to encroached or absent footpaths. The Court directed that footpaths must be provided on all roads, emphasising the state's obligation to ensure safe pedestrian infrastructure. The judgment connects pedestrian safety to the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution.
exam_angle: This ruling expands the judicial interpretation of Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty) to include the right to safe pedestrian movement, relevant for questions on fundamental rights and their evolving scope.
key_facts: Court: Supreme Court of India
Constitutional basis: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Direction: Footpaths must be provided on all roads
Context: Rising pedestrian fatalities in Indian cities
source: The Business Standard / The Indian Express / India Today

title: Iran Claims Closure of Strait of Hormuz Amid Escalating West Asia Tensions
summary: Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, citing Israeli military attacks in Lebanon as the trigger. The Strait of Hormuz is one of the world's most critical maritime chokepoints, through which approximately 20% of global oil trade passes. The move has significant implications for global energy supply chains, oil prices, and India's energy security given its dependence on Gulf oil imports. The development marks a major escalation in the ongoing West Asia conflict.
exam_angle: The Strait of Hormuz is a perennially exam-relevant maritime chokepoint; its closure directly impacts India's energy security, crude oil imports from the Gulf, and broader global trade—relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Energy Security).
key_facts: Location: Strait of Hormuz — between Iran and Oman, connects Persian Gulf to Gulf of Oman
Approximately 20% of global oil trade passes through this strait
Closure cited: Israeli attacks in Lebanon
India imports ~85% of its crude oil, largely from Gulf nations

title: Hindu Minority Protests in Dhaka Over Alleged Desecration of Religious Image
summary: Thousands of protesters gathered in Dhaka, Bangladesh, raising 'Jai Shri Ram' slogans in protest against the alleged desecration of a Hindu religious image. The demonstration reflects ongoing tensions over the safety and rights of the Hindu minority community in Bangladesh following the political transition in 2024. The situation has drawn attention to minority protection issues in Bangladesh and has implications for India-Bangladesh bilateral relations. India has previously expressed concern over attacks on Hindu minorities in Bangladesh.
exam_angle: India-Bangladesh relations and the protection of minority communities in Bangladesh are recurring themes in GS Paper 2 (IR); this event also connects to India's diaspora and neighbourhood-first policy.
key_facts: Location: Dhaka, Bangladesh
Protesters: Hindu minority community
Issue: Alleged desecration of a Hindu religious image
Relevance: India-Bangladesh relations and minority rights in Bangladesh

title: India's Statistical Databases Upgraded: Key Changes Explained
summary: India has undertaken a significant upgrade of its statistical databases, as reported and explained by The Hindu. The upgrades are aimed at improving data quality, coverage, and timeliness of key economic and social indicators used for policy planning. Revisions to databases such as national accounts, employment statistics, and price indices have been under scrutiny amid debates over the accuracy of GDP and other macro-economic data. The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) oversees these statistical systems.
exam_angle: Statistical systems and data quality are relevant for GS Paper 3 (Economy); questions on GDP measurement, the role of MoSPI, and debates around India's economic statistics appear in both Prelims and Mains.
key_facts: Nodal ministry: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI)
Databases upgraded: National accounts, employment, price indices
Objective: Improve data quality, coverage, and timeliness
Context: Ongoing debates on reliability of India's macro-economic statistics

title: US Praises India's GST, Says It Will Boost Bilateral Trade Ties
summary: The United States has expressed a bullish outlook on the Indian economy and specifically praised India's Goods and Services Tax (GST) framework, stating it has the potential to boost trade ties between the two countries. The statement came amid ongoing India-US trade deal negotiations. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal, however, cautioned that a trade deal cannot be implemented until India has a 'competitive advantage' in relevant sectors. The remarks reflect the current state of India-US bilateral trade discussions.
exam_angle: India-US trade relations and the role of GST in facilitating trade are important for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy); the status of India-US bilateral trade negotiations is a frequently tested current affairs topic.
key_facts: US praised India's GST as a facilitator of bilateral trade
Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal: trade deal conditional on India's competitive advantage
Context: Ongoing India-US bilateral trade deal negotiations
GST introduced in India on 1 July 2017 (101st Constitutional Amendment)

title: NSE Highlights Key Risks for India's 2026 Economy: Monsoon, El Niño, and Market Trends
summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) has flagged key macroeconomic risks for India's economy in 2026, including the performance of the monsoon, El Niño weather events, and global market trends. El Niño conditions are associated with below-normal rainfall in India, which adversely affects agricultural output, rural incomes, and food inflation. The NSE report underscores the continued vulnerability of India's economy to climate-related shocks. These risks are critical for monetary policy formulation by the Reserve Bank of India.
exam_angle: The linkage between El Niño, monsoon variability, agricultural output, food inflation, and RBI's monetary policy is a high-value topic for GS Paper 3 (Economy and Environment).
key_facts: Source: National Stock Exchange (NSE) report
Key risks identified: Monsoon performance, El Niño, global market trends
El Niño: Associated with deficit rainfall in India
Impact: Agricultural output, food inflation, rural demand, RBI policy

title: India Must Invest in Deep Tech, Not Rely on Global Trends: Neelkanth Mishra
summary: Neelkanth Mishra, Chief Economist at Axis Bank and member of the Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister (EAC-PM), stated that India must invest strategically in deep technology sectors rather than being dependent on global technology trends. He emphasised that building indigenous deep-tech capabilities is essential for India's long-term economic competitiveness and innovation ecosystem. The statement was made in the context of India's evolving startup and innovation landscape. Deep tech includes areas such as artificial intelligence, semiconductors, space technology, quantum computing, and advanced manufacturing.
exam_angle: India's technology policy, deep tech investments, and the role of the Economic Advisory Council to the PM (EAC-PM) are relevant for GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech and Economy); this also connects to the Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative.
key_facts: Speaker: Neelkanth Mishra — Chief Economist, Axis Bank; member of EAC-PM
Message: India must build indigenous deep-tech capabilities
Deep tech sectors: AI, semiconductors, quantum computing, space, advanced manufacturing
Context: India's startup ecosystem transitioning towards innovation

title: Government Initiatives to Reverse India's Brain Drain: Modi Government's Push
summary: The Modi government has announced a series of initiatives aimed at reversing India's brain drain by attracting Indian talent residing abroad back to the country. These efforts include improving research infrastructure, funding opportunities, and creating an enabling environment for high-skilled professionals in academia, science, and technology. Existing schemes such as the VAIBHAV Fellowship, Pradhan Mantri Fellowship, and ANRF (Anusandhan National Research Foundation) are part of this broader strategy. The initiative also seeks to leverage the Indian diaspora's expertise for national development.
exam_angle: Government schemes targeting the Indian diaspora and reversing brain drain are relevant for GS Paper 2 (Governance and Social Justice); the Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) established under the ANRF Act 2023 is a key Prelims fact.
key_facts: Objective: Reverse brain drain by attracting Indian talent from abroad
Key scheme: VAIBHAV Fellowship (for diaspora scientists)
Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF) — established under ANRF Act 2023
Ministry involved: Ministry of Science and Technology / Ministry of Education

title: Anthropic's Claude Model Temporarily Restricted: AI Governance Concerns
summary: Anthropic's AI model (referred to as 'Fable 5' in some reports) was temporarily restricted and withdrawn from access, raising questions about AI safety, governance, and the self-regulatory practices of leading AI companies. Anthropic is one of the leading AI safety-focused companies globally, backed by major investors including Google and Amazon. The incident highlights the growing importance of AI regulation, safety testing, and responsible deployment frameworks. This comes amid global discussions on AI governance, including at the G7, G20, and the Global Partnership on AI (GPAI).
exam_angle: AI governance, responsible AI deployment, and India's stance on AI regulation (including GPAI membership) are increasingly tested in GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech); the roles of key global AI companies like Anthropic, Google DeepMind, and OpenAI are relevant for current affairs.
key_facts: Company: Anthropic — AI safety-focused company, USA
Event: AI model temporarily restricted/withdrawn over safety concerns
Key backers: Google and Amazon are major investors in Anthropic
Relevance: Global AI governance debate; India is a member of GPAI (Global Partnership on AI)
